sections.title: What is the Third Conditional?
points: It talks about past situations that did not happen.
The structure is: If + past perfect, would have + past participle.
Example: 'If I had remembered, I would have stayed dry.'

sections.title: When to Use It
points: Use it when you imagine a different past outcome.
It often shows regret or missed opportunities.
Example: 'If she had trained, she would have won.'

sections.text: Tip: This conditional does not change the past — it only imagines what could have happened.

cards.fact: The first part of a third conditional sentence.
The result part of a third conditional sentence.
Third conditional often shows a wish for a different past.
